title: Artoria Pendragon (Saber)
content:
Artoria Pendragon, known as Saber, is the Servant summoned by Shirou Emiya in the Fifth Holy Grail War of Fuyuki City. She is the legendary King of Knights of Britain, the wielder of the holy sword Excalibur. Despite being a woman, she ruled as king under a facade of masculinity to prevent civil war.

As a Heroic Spirit, Artoria possesses extremely high parameters across all categories. Her class is Saber, which is considered the most balanced of the seven Servant classes. She has A-rank Magic Resistance, which nullifies almost all magecraft up to two verses.

Her primary Noble Phantasm is Excalibur: Sword of Promised Victory, a divine construct forged by the planet and entrusted to her by the fairy Viviane, the Lady of the Lake. When released, Excalibur converts Artoria's magical energy into pure light and launches it as a blade of destruction, capable of defeating virtually any opponent in a single strike.

Her secondary Noble Phantasm is Avalon: The Ever-Distant Utopia, the sheath of Excalibur which grants its user immortality and near-invulnerability while active. During the events of Fate/stay night, the sheath had been implanted in Kiritsugu Emiya years before, granting Shirou extraordinary regenerative powers.

In the Fate route, Artoria and Shirou form a deep bond as Master and Servant. She struggles with the regret of having sacrificed her humanity for the sake of kingship, and seeks the Holy Grail to undo her mistakes as king. By the end of the Fate route, Shirou helps her accept her life and find peace.

Artoria was also the Saber-class Servant of Kiritsugu Emiya in the Fourth Holy Grail War, depicted in Fate/Zero. In that war, she fought alongside reluctant partnership with the mercenary known as the Magus Killer, whose utilitarian approach to the War clashed with her ideals of chivalry.

title: EMIYA (Archer)
content:
The Servant known as Archer in the Fifth Holy Grail War is the Heroic Spirit EMIYA — a Counter Guardian who was once a human named Shirou Emiya from an alternate future. He was summoned by Rin Tohsaka due to a Command Spell mishap, forming an unorthodox Master-Servant pair.

Archer's true identity is kept secret for much of Fate/stay night, though it becomes central to the Unlimited Blade Works route. Having made a contract with the world (Alaya) in life to save people in exchange for becoming a tool of the Counter Force after death, he became an Heroic Spirit without a name recorded in the Throne of Heroes. He is summoned through an incomplete ritual, lacking a true name and appearing with white hair and tanned skin — symptoms of excessive use of magical energy projection.

His unique Noble Phantasm is Unlimited Blade Works: Infinite Creation of Swords, an innate Reality Marble that manifests a Reality Marble from within his soul. The inner world contains every weapon he has ever recorded with his eyes, along with the hills and gears that represent his nature as a sword. He can reproduce any weapon he has seen, albeit at a slightly reduced rank.

Archer wields twin blades: Kanshou and Bakuya, married swords forged in Chinese legend. He can use Projection to create multiple sets, throwing them to overwhelm opponents. He is also capable of projecting Caladbolg II and using it as an arrow — the Broken Phantasm technique — to devastate targets at range.

Unlike most Servants who fight directly, Archer prefers to maintain distance, using Reinforcement and Projection to augment arrows and rain destruction from afar. His greatest skill is Tracing: creating perfect copies of Noble Phantasms from recordings stored in his Reality Marble.

title: Cu Chulainn (Lancer)
content:
Cu Chulainn is the Lancer-class Servant in the Fifth Holy Grail War, summoned by Bazett Fraga McRemitz and later contracted to Kirei Kotomine after Kirei betrayed Bazett. He is the legendary hero of Celtic mythology, the Child of Light, son of the god Lugh.

Cu Chulainn is regarded as one of the most physically capable Servants in the war, possessing A-rank Agility that allows him to move faster than the eye can follow. His Battle Continuation skill of A-rank means he can continue fighting even after receiving wounds that would be fatal to ordinary beings.

His Noble Phantasm is Gáe Bolg: Spear of Striking Death Flight, a cursed spear made from the bone of a sea monster. Gáe Bolg has two applications:
1. A thrown anti-army technique that causes the spear to split into thorns that pierce everything in its path.
2. A thrusting technique that reverses causality — the spear's activation guarantees that it has already pierced the target's heart, circumventing normal defensive logic. The only defense is to avoid the attack entirely before it can be initiated.

Cu Chulainn is characterized by his cheerful and battle-loving personality. Despite serving Kirei — whom he dislikes — he follows orders and fights with straightforward ferocity. He has a strong sense of honor and respects worthy opponents.

In the Fate route, he fights Saber multiple times and is ultimately ordered to kill Shirou, a task he finds distasteful. In the Unlimited Blade Works route, he is assassinated by Caster's Rule Breaker, having his contract forcibly severed. In the Heaven's Feel route, he is one of the Servants consumed by the Shadow.

title: Gilgamesh (Archer, Fate/Zero and Fate/stay night)
content:
Gilgamesh is the Archer-class Servant of Tokiomi Tohsaka in the Fourth Holy Grail War (Fate/Zero) and later an independent Servant — turned human — in the Fifth Holy Grail War (Fate/stay night). He is the King of Heroes, the world's oldest hero recorded in the Epic of Gilgamesh, who ruled the ancient Babylonian city-state of Uruk.

Gilgamesh is generally considered the most powerful Servant in terms of raw Noble Phantasm firepower. He possesses Gate of Babylon: King's Treasure, a Noble Phantasm that functions as a key to his vault, which contains the original prototypes of every Noble Phantasm in existence. All legendary weapons humanity created are derived from his collection, making him the 'owner' of all Noble Phantasms.

His most powerful weapon within Gate of Babylon is Ea: Sword of Rupture — Enuma Elish, an Anti-World Noble Phantasm classified as a divine construct that predates human history. Ea generates a wind pressure tunnel by rotating its three segments at high speed, separating space itself. When fully released, Enuma Elish recreates the moment of the world's birth and can literally split reality.

Gilgamesh possesses the Golden Rule skill at A-rank, granting him absurd wealth. His Charisma is also A-rank, granting extraordinary influence over others. His class skill Independent Action allows him to operate without a Master for extended periods.

Despite his overwhelming power, Gilgamesh refuses to use his full arsenal against opponents he deems unworthy, playing with them rather than ending fights immediately. This arrogance is his greatest weakness — he was defeated by Rider (Iskandar) in Fate/Zero when Iskandar briefly summoned his Reality Marble, and was destroyed by Shirou's Unlimited Blade Works in the fifth war.

title: Medusa (Rider)
content:
Medusa is the Rider-class Servant in the Fifth Holy Grail War, initially contracted to Shinji Matou but later to Sakura Matou. She is the legendary Gorgon of Greek mythology, though her appearance in Fate/stay night portrays her as one of three sisters — the Gorgon sisters — who existed before she transformed into the monster of myth.

Despite being placed in the Rider class (which typically indicates weaker close-combat stats), Medusa is an extraordinarily powerful Servant. She wields a Nameless Dagger (Harpe) — a cursed blade that negates divine protections — and uses Breaker Gorgon, a mystic eye-sealing item, to suppress her godlike Mystic Eyes of Petrification that she was born with.

Her primary Noble Phantasm is Bellerophon: Bridle of Chivalry, which she uses by riding the divine horse Pegasus. Pegasus, itself a divine spirit, serves as her mount and can attain supersonic speed. When Bellerophon is released, Medusa and Pegasus charge with explosive power amplification, becoming capable of destroying entire defensive structures.

Her other Noble Phantasm is Blood Fort Andromeda: Outer-Locking Curse of the Blood, which creates a bounded field that drains prana from anyone trapped within it, converting them into magical energy she can absorb.

In the Heaven's Feel route, Medusa undergoes a significant transformation. As Sakura is consumed by the cursed Holy Grail, she summons an evolved form of Medusa — the true Gorgon — representing what Medusa became after being corrupted by humanity's fear of her beauty. In this form, she is nearly unrecognizable from the calm, stoic Servant seen earlier in the story.
